name: MCP Cloudflare Server
description: Official Cloudflare catalog of managed remote MCP servers, connected over OAuth. The primary Cloudflare API server at https://mcp.cloudflare.com/mcp exposes 2,500+ API endpoints (DNS, Workers, R2, Zero Trust, and more) through two Code Mode tools, search() and execute(), in roughly 1,000 tokens. Sixteen additional product-specific servers cover documentation, Workers bindings and builds, observability, Radar, browser rendering, AI Gateway, audit logs, and more.

evidence.value: Built on Cloudflare's highly reliable API with 99.95%+ uptime
High success rate for DNS, CDN, and Workers operations
Respects Cloudflare rate limits (1200 requests/5 minutes)
Excellent global performance with 300+ edge locations
Handles API errors with retry logic and fallback
Managed remote servers use an OAuth flow where the user is redirected to Cloudflare to authorize and select permissions; API token bearer auth remains available for CI/CD

authentication_security.methodology: Authentication mechanism review; raised from 80 as the default connection model is now OAuth with explicit permission selection rather than static API tokens

evidence.value: Remote OAuth connections avoid storing long-lived API tokens in client configuration; token exposure risk remains for CI/CD bearer-token setups

token_exposure_risk.methodology: Token security analysis; raised from 65 because the remote OAuth default removes locally stored long-lived tokens

evidence.value: AI can modify DNS records, potentially causing service disruption
Can modify firewall rules, WAF settings, and security policies
All operations logged in Cloudflare audit system
Token scoping limits zone access but requires careful configuration
Zone configurations, DNS records, and Workers code sent to LLM provider
Domain names, origins, and infrastructure details may be exposed
May access traffic analytics and user behavior data
Infrastructure data shared with LLM provider per their privacy policy
Workers code may contain secrets or sensitive configuration
Comprehensive documentation with Cloudflare-specific examples
All operations logged in Cloudflare audit trail and MCP logs
Open source implementation from Cloudflare
Official docs enumerate every managed server with its endpoint URL and purpose; SSE transport is deprecated in favor of streamable-http at /mcp paths

api_coverage_clarity.methodology: API documentation review; raised from 72 as the current docs enumerate the full managed server catalog

evidence.value: Zero-install: add the managed server URL (e.g. https://mcp.cloudflare.com/mcp) in an OAuth-capable MCP client and authorize; no local server or API token required for interactive use

ease_of_setup.methodology: Setup complexity assessment; raised from 82 as managed remote servers eliminate local installation and token provisioning

evidence.value: Excellent API performance (typically 50-200ms globally)
Highly reliable with 99.95%+ uptime

Primary API server exposes 2,500+ endpoints via Code Mode search()/execute(); 16 product-specific servers cover docs, bindings, builds, observability, Radar, containers, browser rendering, Logpush, AI Gateway, AI Search, audit logs, DNS analytics, DEX, CASB, and GraphQL

service_coverage.methodology: Feature coverage assessment; raised from 78 as coverage expanded to essentially the full Cloudflare API plus a broad server catalog

evidence.value: Growing adoption among Cloudflare users and edge developers; Cloudflare also publishes templates and skills for building remote MCP servers on Workers

code-generation.notes: Excellent for Workers code generation and infrastructure-as-code

customer-support.notes: Useful for troubleshooting edge infrastructure and CDN issues

content-creation.notes: Moderate applicability for CDN and Pages documentation

data-analysis.notes: Good for analyzing traffic patterns, performance metrics, and security data

research-assistant.notes: Useful for researching edge computing patterns and Cloudflare configurations

legal-compliance.notes: Risk of exposing infrastructure details; careful access control needed

healthcare.notes: Risk of exposing infrastructure for regulated healthcare applications

financial-analysis.notes: Moderate risk for financial infrastructure management

education.notes: Excellent for teaching edge computing, CDN, and serverless concepts

creative-writing.notes: Low relevance to creative writing workflows

best_for: Edge computing developers building Cloudflare Workers applications
DevOps engineers managing CDN configurations and security policies
Educators teaching serverless and edge computing concepts
strengths: Official Cloudflare managed remote MCP servers with OAuth and per-connection permission selection
Token-efficient Code Mode: 2,500+ API endpoints reachable through search()/execute() in about 1,000 tokens
Broad catalog of 16 product-specific servers (docs, observability, Radar, bindings, audit logs, and more)
Excellent global API performance on Cloudflare's edge network
Full operation auditability through Cloudflare audit logs
Open source implementations with Cloudflare support; high reliability with 99.95%+ uptime
limitations: Infrastructure configurations and Workers code exposed to LLM provider
Risk of DNS manipulation causing service disruption
Can modify security policies and firewall rules within authorized permissions
Domain names and infrastructure details may be disclosed
Workers code may contain secrets or sensitive configuration
execute() on the primary API server can reach any authorized endpoint, so OAuth permission selection must be scoped carefully; CI/CD bearer tokens still need manual scoping

supported_platforms: Any OAuth-capable MCP client (remote managed servers); self-hostable on Cloudflare Workers
metadata.authentication: OAuth (interactive, with permission selection) or Cloudflare API token bearer auth (CI/CD)
